data.title: Hick’s Law
data.description: Design focuses on reducing decision-making time by minimizing choices.
data.technical_spec: Implementation: Simple navigation, prioritized content flow.

data.title: Jakob’s Law
data.description: Users prefer interfaces familiar to their own experiences from other sites.
data.technical_spec: Standards: Consistent iconography, similar navigation patterns.

data.title: Fitts’s Law
data.description: Design with larger, more accessible target elements for a more user-friendly experience.
data.technical_spec: Approach: Enlarged buttons, accessible links with enough space.

data.title: Miller’s Law
data.description: The idea that users can only hold 7±2 pieces of information in their working memory.

data.title: Tesler’s Law
data.description: Available simplicity in user interfaces minimizes complexity for users.
